commands.review_summary:
The PR expands sandbox Docker env config values to accept SecretInput/SecretRef values, resolves active defaults/per-agent env refs through the secrets runtime, registers configure/audit targets, and updates tests/docs.

PR surface: Source +197, Tests +249, Docs +16. Total +462 across 16 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the requested capability: current main still accepts only string Docker env values and has no sandbox Docker env SecretRef registry targets. I did not run a live current-main Docker repro in this read-only pass.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is maintainer/security acceptance of the Docker env credential exposure boundary, not a narrow automated repair.

target.title: [codex] feat(sandbox): support SecretRef docker env
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`sandbox.docker.env` previously accepted only literal strings. Operators who needed API keys, database URLs, or other credentials inside Docker sandbox containers had to put plaintext into `openclaw.json` or rely on ad-hoc environment interpolation outside the SecretRef flow. That left sandbox env credential handling inconsistent with model providers, skills, gateway auth, and other config-backed credential surfaces.

## Summary

- allow `agents.defaults.sandbox.docker.env.*` and `agents.list[].sandbox.docker.env.*` values to be SecretInput strings or structured SecretRefs
- resolve active Docker sandbox env SecretRefs during runtime snapshot preparation while leaving inactive sandbox surfaces unresolved with diagnostics
- include the sandbox Docker env targets in the direct `openclaw agent` command SecretRef detector and agent runtime target set
- preserve existing literal Docker env string behavior exactly, including empty-string values like `FOO=`
- register the new sandbox env credential surfaces for secrets plan/configure/audit and refresh the SecretRef credential docs matrix

Fixes #38309

## Security Note

Resolved SecretRefs in `sandbox.docker.env` still become Docker container environment variables. That matches the existing Docker env behavior and keeps the docs warning that these values are visible through Docker daemon/container metadata; maintainer/security acceptance is still required for that boundary.

## Evidence

Redacted real Docker proof from this branch:

```text
agent target ids include sandbox env: agents.defaults.sandbox.docker.env.*, agents.list[].sandbox.docker.env.*
command secret diagnostics: 1
resolved config env string: true len=29 sha256=127866ee3204
sandbox docker env normalized: true len=29 sha256=127866ee3204
empty literal preserved: true
sandbox container created: oc-sref-agent-main-f331f052
container env present=True len=29 sha256=127866ee3204 empty_literal=True
sandbox container removed: oc-sref-agent-main-f331f052
```

Validation:

- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cli/command-secret-targets.test.ts src/commands/agent.runtime-config.test.ts src/secrets/runtime.test.ts src/secrets/runtime.coverage.test.ts src/config/config.sandbox-docker.test.ts src/agents/sandbox-merge.test.ts src/agents/sandbox-create-args.test.ts` passed: 5 Vitest shards, 177 tests total
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 ...` passed for touched files
- `pnpm lint --threads=8` passed
- `pnpm tsgo:core` passed
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test` passed
